blockingIssues.description: The ingest milestone contract still requires an end-to-end `schema SQL -> extract from cassava -> ingest -> verify -> delete -> re-ingest -> verify` flow, but `test/test_ingest_microct.py` continues to seed synthetic fixture dicts instead of exercising `extract_microct.py`, leaving `VAL-CROSS-002` unproven and masking concrete interface mismatches between extractor output and ingester input.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented in `.factory/library/environment.md` that public Cassava access exposes metadata JSONs but individual MicroCT CSV/GraphML file bodies require Pennsieve-authenticated access.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update `db-pipeline-worker` guidance to warn that public Cassava access exposes metadata JSON but not the underlying CSV/GraphML file bodies needed for live MicroCT ingest tests, so workers should plan authenticated access or explicitly renegotiate end-to-end test expectations before substituting synthetic fixtures.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `ingest-pipeline-and-tests` handoff documents a deviation to synthetic data because the needed file bodies were unavailable through public Cassava, and the scrutiny review confirmed that this deviation left `VAL-CROSS-002` untested while hiding extractor→ingester schema mismatches.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reconcile the shared lint guidance so the mission instructions and `.factory/services.yaml` no longer disagree about whether flake8 should be run in this environment.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `.factory/services.yaml` still advertises a flake8 lint command, while `AGENTS.md` says flake8 is currently broken and should be skipped.
